title: iberis sempervirens subsp. pseudosaxatilis
intro: Iberis sempervirens subsp. pseudosaxatilis, also known as Rock Cress or Alpine Rock-cress, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of Iberis sempervirens subsp. pseudosaxatilis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
